name: Celtic Knotwork
shortDescription: Celtic knotwork is a decorative art form characterized by intricate, unbroken lines that weave in and out of themselves, symbolizing eternity, interconnectedness, and the cyclical nature of life.
description: Celtic knotwork is a distinctive feature of Celtic art and design, appearing in a wide range of contexts from illuminated manuscripts like the Book of Kells to stone carvings and metalwork. The unbroken lines of the knots are often interpreted as representing eternity, the interconnectedness of all things, and the cyclical nature of life, death, and rebirth. The absence of a clear beginning or end point emphasizes the continuous flow of existence and the interwoven relationship between the physical and spiritual realms. Different knot patterns may have held specific meanings, although much of the original symbolism has been lost to time. Triquetras, triskeles, and other recurring motifs are frequently incorporated into knotwork designs, further enriching their symbolic depth. The complex interlacing patterns also reflect the intricate nature of the Celtic worldview, which emphasized the importance of relationships, community, and the balance of natural forces. The visual appeal of Celtic knotwork lies in its intricate complexity and the sense of movement created by the interwoven lines. Its enduring popularity reflects its aesthetic beauty and its profound symbolic resonance with fundamental human experiences.

sources.title: Celtic Art: From Its Beginnings to the Book of Kells
sources.author: Ruth Megaw and Vincent Megaw
